review_summary: Transcript is sufficient for reinforcement questions about neural implicit fields, NeRF, differentiable volume rendering, and Gaussian splatting. The conceptual dependencies are verbalized clearly, so no clip is needed.

question_bank.question: Compared with earlier deep implicit geometry methods, what extra input does NeRF use to model appearance?
choices: Viewing direction along with spatial position
question_bank.explanation: Appearance depends on view direction, so NeRF augments the 3D query location with camera-direction information when predicting radiance.

question_bank.question: Why can NeRF be trained from 2D images rather than requiring direct 3D supervision?
choices: Because its rendering procedure is differentiable with respect to density and color predictions
Because it converts every image into a mesh before training
Because camera direction is ignored during optimization
Because all rays sample only one 3D point
question_bank.explanation: Differentiable volume rendering lets gradients flow from image reconstruction errors back into the neural field’s density and color predictions along sampled rays.

question_bank.question: Why is 3D Gaussian splatting typically much faster to render than NeRF?
choices: It ignores appearance and predicts only occupancy
It focuses computation on sparse occupied regions instead of sampling many empty points along rays
It replaces 3D geometry with 2D segmentation masks
question_bank.explanation: Gaussian splatting represents the scene with sparse blobs where matter exists, so rendering avoids much of the wasted sampling that slows volumetric ray marching.
